author: Augustine of Hippo
book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.

classification.cautionReason: No major mainstream heresy warning is attached by the preview classifier.

lineage: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
title: Continuation of the Preceding.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But why do we not in opposition to this, rather hear the words, “Who hath first given to Him and it shall be recompensed to him again? since of Him, and through Him, and in Him, are all things.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And from whom, then, is that very beginning of our faith if not from Him?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For this is not excepted when other things are spoken of as of Him; but “of Him, and through Him, and in Him, are all things.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: But who can say that he who has already begun to believe deserves nothing from Him in whom he has believed?

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Whence it results that, to him who already deserves, other things are said to be added by a divine retribution, and thus that God’s grace is given according to our merits.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And this assertion when put before him, Pelagius himself condemned, that he might not be condemned.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Whoever, then, wishes on every side to avoid this condemnable opinion, let him understand that what the apostle says is said with entire truthfulness, “Unto you it is given in the behalf of Christ not only to believe on Him, but also to suffer for His sake.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: He shows that both are the gifts of God, because he said that both were given.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: And he does not say, “to believe on Him more fully and perfectly,” but, “to believe on Him.”

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Neither does he say that he himself had obtained mercy to be more faithful, but “to be faithful,” because he knew that he had not first given the beginning of his faith to God, and had its increase given back to him again by Him; but that he had been made faithful by God, who also had made him an apostle.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: For the beginnings of his faith are recorded, and they are very well known by being read in the church on an occasion calculated to distinguish them: how, being turned away from the faith which he was destroying, and being vehemently opposed to it, he was suddenly by a more powerful grace converted to it, by the conversion of Him, to whom as One who would do this very thing it was said by the prophet, “Thou wilt turn and quicken us;” so that not only from one who refused to believe he was made a willing believer, but, moreover, from being a persecutor, he suffered persecution in defence of that faith which he persecuted.

verses.book: A Treatise on the Predestination of the Saints.
verses.text: Because it was given him by Christ “not only to believe on Him, but also to suffer for His sake.”
